item.title: Alexis Ohanian’s Next Social Platform Has One Rule: Don’t Act Like an Asshole
item.description: The Reddit cofounder joins WIRED’s “Uncanny Valley” podcast and opens up about his early days in tech, his plans for Digg, the future of women’s sports, and what his immigrant mom taught him about America.
item.media:keywords: Big Interview, Uncanny Valley Podcast, podcasts, reddit, Social Media, artificial intelligence
item.dc:creator: Katie Drummond
item.dc:subject: Dad Mod

item.title: AI Is Eliminating Jobs for Younger Workers
item.description: New research from Stanford provides the clearest available evidence that AI is reshaping the workforce—but it’s complicated.
item.media:keywords: AI Lab, artificial intelligence, research, chatbots, Work, Silicon Valley, machine learning
item.dc:creator: Will Knight
item.dc:subject: AI Lab

item.title: Elon Musk’s xAI Sues Apple and OpenAI Over App Store Rankings
item.description: The xAI lawsuit claims that Grok’s ranking below ChatGPT is a sign of allegedly monopolistic behavior.
item.media:keywords: apple, Elon Musk, artificial intelligence, xAI, App Store, Apps, OpenAI, ChatGPT
item.dc:creator: Zoë Schiffer
item.dc:subject: Ranked Choice

item.title: IBM and NASA Develop a Digital Twin of the Sun to Predict Future Solar Storms
item.description: The tool models the sun using AI, and its developers say it can anticipate solar flares 16 percent more accurately and in half the time of current prediction systems.
item.media:keywords: artificial intelligence, NASA, IBM, Sun, space, science
item.dc:creator: Fernanda González
item.dc:subject: Weather Forecasting

item.title: The Hidden Ingredients Behind AI’s Creativity
item.description: Image generators are designed to mimic their training data, so where does their apparent creativity come from? A recent study suggests that it’s an inevitable by-product of their architecture.
item.media:keywords: Quanta Magazine, computer science, artificial intelligence, creativity, machine learning
item.dc:creator: Webb Wright
item.dc:subject: Creative Genius

item.title: The Trump-Intel Deal Is Official
item.description: The $8.9 billion investment gives the US government a roughly 10 percent equity stake in Intel.
item.media:keywords: Intel, Donald Trump, politics, chips, manufacturing
item.dc:creator: Lauren Goode
item.dc:subject: Chip Change

item.title: Join Our Next Livestream: Back to School in the Age of AI
item.description: Bring your stories and questions on August 28 as we explore our latest investigations into how tech is shaping education today.
item.media:keywords: webinar, education, learning, technology, artificial intelligence, Generation iPad, kids, Social Media
item.dc:subject: Livestream Q&A

item.title: Meet Wukong, the AI Chatbot China Has Installed on Its Space Station
item.description: China has rolled out a chatbot on its Tiangong space station. Its mission: to improve safety, navigation, and coordination in orbit.
item.media:keywords: space, artificial intelligence, China, chatbots, science
item.dc:creator: Jorge Garay
item.dc:subject: Space Odyssey?

item.title: Why Did a $10 Billion Startup Let Me Vibe-Code for Them—and Why Did I Love It?
item.description: I spent two days at Notion and saw an industry in upheaval. I also shipped some actual code.
item.media:keywords: longreads, artificial intelligence, Startups, coding, Jobs
item.dc:creator: Lauren Goode
item.dc:subject: Code Monkey

item.title: Do Large Language Models Dream of AI Agents?
item.description: For AI models, knowing what to remember might be as important as knowing what to forget. Welcome to the era of “sleeptime compute.”
item.media:keywords: AI Lab, artificial intelligence, chatbots, machine learning, algorithms, ai
item.dc:creator: Will Knight
item.dc:subject: AI Lab
